rulings.Case Title: DRIP DROP INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. SOS HYDRATION INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Defendants To Remove Attorneys Eyes Only Designation From Plaintiffs C.C.P. Section 2019.210 Disclosure Of Alleged Trade Secrets
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 1, 2015, Line 1, PART 1 OF TENTATIVE RULING : DEFENDANTS SOS HYDRATION INC.,, BLANCA LIZAOLA, JAMES MAYO, MICHAEL PAGE INTERNATIONAL, INC. Notice Of Motion And Motion By Defendants To Remove Attorneys Eyes Only Designation From Plaintiffs C.C.P. Section 2019.210 Disclosure Of Alleged Trade Secrets Pro Tem Judge Douglas Robbins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Movants and Defendants SOS Hydration, Inc., Blanca Lizaola, James Mayo and Michael Page International Inc. (collectively "Defendants") Motion to Remove "Attorneys Eyes Only" Designation from Plaintiff's C.C.P. § 2019.210 Disclosure of Alleged Trade Secrets is GRANTED. Having failed to notice the request for monetary sanctions, and having failed to note the request for sanctions on the caption page as required under the Code, Defendants' request for monetary sanctions against Plaintiff Drip Drop, Inc. ("Drip Drop") is DENIED. Although there is an extensive discussion in the briefing as to the meaning of Cal. Civ. Proc. Code § 2019.210 which requires plaintiffs alleging a trade secret to make a showing of that trade secret with "reasonable particularity" before discovery may begin, the Instant Motion does not ask this Court to adjudicate the question as to whether Drip Drop has or has not sufficiently complied with section 2019.210, and the Court declines to make that determination. Instead the somewhat narrow issue is whether the documents attached to Drip Drop's Amended Further Disclosure of Trade Secrets of May 5, 2015 (the "TS Disclosure"), should be stripped of their Attorneys Eyes Only ("AEO") designation. Drip Drop opposes the Motion arguing that removing the AEO from the documents at issue is not necessary and is the result of the Parties' own fully negotiated and stipulated Protective Order. But the Protective Order itself states that when a party challenges an AEO designation, the burden of proving the necessity of the designation is placed on the party making the designation-which in this case is Drip Drop itself. Stip. and PO 10. Drip Drop has failed to meet this burden.

rulings.Case Title: DRIP DROP INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. SOS HYDRATION INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Defendants To Remove Attorneys Eyes Only Designation From Plaintiffs C.C.P. Section 2019.210 Disclosure Of Alleged Trade Secrets
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 1, 2015, Line 1, PART 2 OF TENTATIVE RULING - DEFENDANTS SOS HYDRATION INC.,, BLANCA LIZAOLA, JAMES MAYO and MICHAEL PAGE INTERNATIONAL, INC. Notice Of Motion And Motion By Defendants To Remove Attorneys Eyes Only Designation From Plaintiffs C.C.P. Section 2019.210 Disclosure Of Alleged Trade Secrets Drip Drop argues that de-designating AEO from the TS Disclosure documents would put Drip Drop at a "severe competitive disadvantage" as against its rival in the market, SOS Hydration and other Defendants. But even in the event of AEO de-designation, the documents at issue would continue to be-and shall be by virtue of this Order-protected by a "Confidential" designation which should be sufficient. Drip Drop has failed to explain why a mere "Confidential" designation is unlikely to be effective in deterring Defendants from using the alleged trade secrets at issue. Moreover, whatever (unquantified) risk of competitive disadvantage Drip Drop might suffer is outweighed by Defendants' right under law and constitutional law to put up a defense in the Instant Case. Defendants provide a number of persuasive examples in Reply as to how the AEO designation prevents their attorneys from meaningfully interacting with them for purposes of defending themselves by trying to prove that the alleged trade secrets at issue are not secrets at all. Whether the designated information really is or is not a trade secret is not the subject of adjudication here, and the Court refrains from making any such decree at this time. But either way, the prevailing rule remains: absent significant countervailing reasons, attorneys must be able to communicate with their own clients regarding important evidence relevant to taking discovery and mounting a defense. Defendants shall prepare a form of the order consistent with this tentative ruling and shall bring a copy of the proposed order to the hearing, if any. In the absence of a hearing, the prevailing party shall timely comply with Cal. R. Ct. 3.1312. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to drobbins@woodrobbins.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: COLETTE HUGHES VS. MICHAEL WYNTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Claims By Plaintiff Against Defendants Michael Wynter And Sarah Wynter
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 1, 2015, Line 13, PLAINTIFF COLETTE HUGHES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Claims By Plaintiff Against Defendants Michael Wynter And Sarah Wynter. Denied. Summary adjudication is denied as to the second, third, and fourth issues (as set forth in the Notice of Motion) because there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether Defendants misrepresented to Plaintiff that they were licensed. (Declaration of Sarah Wynter, par. 2; Declaration of Michael Wynter, par. 2.) Summary adjudication is denied as to the first issue because the applicable statute of limitations bars the claim. Plaintiff seeks to recover pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code sec. 7031. Plaintiff's right to recover solely arises from this statute, and therefore, the three year statute of limitations set forth in CCP sec. 338 applies. A cause of action accrues "upon the occurrence of the last element essential to the cause of action." (Howard Jarvis Taxpayers Association v. City of La Habra (2001) 25 Cal.4th 809, 815.) Plaintiff's cause of action accrued when she made her first payment to Defendants on December 31, 2010. (Plaintiff's Compendium of Evidence, Ex. 4.) The complaint was filed on May 23, 2014 and thus, recovery on at least some of the payments are barred by the statute of limitations. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JAN TOLU VS. THE CELLAR, A BUSINESS ENTITY OF UNKNOWN FORM DOIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication (As To Jan Tolu)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 1, 2015, Line 12, DEFENDANT THE CELLAR NIGHTCLUB, LLC, AS SUCCESSOR TO LELAND VENTURES, LLC DBA THE CELLAR'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication As To Jan Tolu. The court grants plaintiff's motion to amend the complaint and takes the motion for summary judgment as to plaintiff's complaint off calendar as moot. See State Compensation Insurance Fund v. Sup. Crt. (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1124, 1130-1131 (amended complaint moots pending summary judgment motion). The court continues the trial date to December 7, 2015, to insure that needed discovery can go forward and defendant can renew its motion for summary judgment if necessary. The case is still in its early stages and this is the first trial continuance. The court denies defendant's motion for summary judgment/adjudication on the cross complaint. Defendant failed to maintain its initial burden of production and the motion is premature. Liability has not been established and there is no judgment. "The obligation of indemnity [is] "the obligation resting on one party to make good a loss or damage another has incurred." E.E. White, Inc. v. City of Huntington Beach (1978) 21 Cal.3d 497, 506; see also Herrero v. Atkinson (1964) 227 Cal.App.2d 69, 74 ("The duty to indemnify may arise, and indemnity may be allowed in those fact situations where in equity and good conscience the burden of the judgment should be shifted from the shoulders of the person seeking indemnity to the one from whom indemnity is sought.") (emphasis added). The motion does not comply with the CRC. The notice of motion and separate statement are not entirely clear exactly what should be adjudicated. The notice of motion refers to adjudicating the causes of action, but it also seeks a determination that defendant is not at fault and SIS must defend the action. The separate statement does not comply with CRC 3.1350. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 1, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANT BURR PILGER MAYER, INC. Defendant Burr Pilger Mayer Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Pro Tem Judge Douglas Robbins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Pro Tem Discovery Judge recuses himself from this action, as he and his firm has represented Defendant Burr Pilger Mayer Inc. and one of its shareholders in the past. This Motion is continued to be heard by another Pro Tem Discovery Judge on June 15, 2015, at 9:00 a.m. in room 301. rulings.Case Title: ROSEMARY THAKER VS. POTRERO HEALTHE CENTER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 1, 2015, Line 7, DEFENDANT ANDREA NATTA, L.A.C'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained. The demurrer to the second cause of action for Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff concedes that this cause of action is duplicative of the negligence cause of action. The demurrer to the third cause of action for Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. The complaint does not allege facts establishing extreme or outrageous behavior.
